title: AN ACT SEPARATING THE STA. MARGARITA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL ГўВЂВ“ NAPURO ANNEX IN BARANGAY NAPURO, MUNICIPALITY OF STA. MARGARITA, PROVINCE OF SAMAR FROM THE STA. MARGARITA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, CONVERTING IT INTO AN INDEPENDENT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL TO BE KNOWN AS NAPURO NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

chunks.text:
108 OG No. 50, 6324 (December 10, 2012 )

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 10222, October 18, 2012 ]

AN ACT SEPARATING THE STA. MARGARITA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL ГўВЂВ“ NAPURO ANNEX IN BARANGAY NAPURO, MUNICIPALITY OF STA. MARGARITA, PROVINCE OF SAMAR FROM THE STA. MARGARITA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, CONVERTING IT INTO AN INDEPENDENT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL TO BE KNOWN AS NAPURO NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1.

Separation and Conversion into a National High School.

ГўВЂВ“ The Sta. Margarita National High School ГўВЂВ“ Napuro Annex in Barangay Napuro, Municipality of Sta. Margarita, Province of Samar is hereby separated from the Sta. Margarita National High School and converted into an independent national high school to be known as Napuro National High School.

SEC. 2.

Transfer of Assets and Liabilities.

ГўВЂВ“ All personnel, assets, liabilities and records of the Sta. Margarita National High School ГўВЂВ“ Napuro Annex are hereby transferred to and absorbed by the Napuro National High School.

SEC. 3.

Appropriations. ГўВЂВ“

The Secretary of Education shall immediately include in the DepartmentГўВЂВ™s program the operationalization of the Napuro National High School, the initial funding of which shall be charged against the current yearГўВЂВ™s appropriations of the Sta. Margarita National High School ГўВЂВ“ Napuro Annex. Thereafter, the amount necessary for the continued operation of the school shall be included in the annual General Appropriations Act.

SEC. 4.

Implementation. ГўВЂВ“

The Secretary of Education shall issue such rules and regulations as may be necessary to carry out the purpose of this Act.

SEC. 5.

Effectivity. ГўВЂВ“

This Act shall take effect fifteen (15) days after its publication in the

Official Gazette.

Approved,

(Sgd.) JUAN PONCE ENRILE

President of the Senate

(Sgd.)

FELICIANO BELMONTE JR.

Speaker of the House

of Representatives

This Act which originated in the House of Representatives was finally passed by the House of Representatives and the Senate on October 3, 2011 and July 24, 2012, respectively.

(Sgd.) EMMA LIRIO-REYES

Secretary of the Senate

(Sgd.) MARILYN B. BARUA-YAP

Secretary General

House of Representatives

Approved:

OCT 18 2012

(Sgd.) BENIGNO S. AQUINO III

President of the Philippines
